items.criterion: `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` exists and exports JSDoc-documented public actions/selectors for ordered overlay registration, topmost queries, stack-derived layering, and element/context bridge helpers, while remaining a small dedicated lifecycle store.

The implementation explicitly contains Base UI's built-in focus/body ownership so the overlay stack is the single automated owner of `document.body.style.pointerEvents` and focus restoration for modal overlays after wrapper composition.
Overlay registration is tied to actual open/present popup or backdrop lifecycle rather than long-lived root mount state.

Tooltip and preview-card are treated as non-modal ordering-only overlays: they receive stack layering/topmost identity but do not capture `restoreFocusTo`, do not restore focus on close, and do not affect body pointer-events ownership.

An explicit overlay-identity bridge exists under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` so ref-based dialog callers and callback-only lightbox hooks can consume topmost state without bespoke prop threading.

No raw `@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card|drawer)` imports or mocks remain anywhere under `src/` outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`, including type-only imports and the current tooltip test mock.

`LightboxShell.tsx` no longer imports `@base-ui/react/dialog` directly and instead consumes a sanctioned overlay-aware wrapper path under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` that preserves its modal semantics, dismissal-cancellation contract, custom event handlers, and marker exposure.
The existing Base UI `preview-card` wrapper is migrated into the centralized overlay ownership layer so no stack-relevant Base UI portal primitive remains outside M2.

The repository makes explicit, verified N/A decisions about `ContextMenu` and `Drawer`: both `@base-ui/react/context-menu` and `@base-ui/react/drawer` remain absent under `src/` before and after the migration, and the bespoke `ToolsPaneDrawer` stays out of scope as a non-Base-UI overlay.

Menu migration explicitly covers both popup branches in `src/shared/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx`: `DropdownMenuContent` and `DropdownMenuSubContent` both register with the overlay stack and neither retains a hardcoded numeric layering path.

DOM/z-index precedence helpers are retired for migrated overlay flows: `uiLayers.ts`, lightbox interaction/navigation hooks, and `LoraSelectorModal` no longer determine topmost state by querying DOM markers or comparing computed z-index values.

`useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts` no longer contains `targetHasHigherZIndex()` or any equivalent computed-style z-index gate; lightbox precedence is determined only through overlay-stack identity/topmost queries.

Helper-based container and popup-overlay resolution is centralized and preserved: `CommunityLorasTab.tsx`, `MyLorasTab.tsx`, `sharedSections.tsx`, `useSlidingPane.ts`, `GuidanceVideoStrip.tsx`, `elementPolicy.ts`, `useMobileInteractions.ts`, and `useLightboxTransition.ts` use shared overlay helpers instead of open-coded marker queries.

Backdrop-marker and popup-marker compatibility remain explicit and intact during M2 across both JS and CSS support surfaces, including `src/index.css`, `src/styles/components/interactions.css`, `src/styles/components/dialog-overrides.css`, `src/styles/components/wes-surfaces.css`, and `src/styles/foundations/base-case.css`.

Caller-side layering escape hatches for migrated Base UI overlay consumers are removed or neutralized, including indirect forwarded-prop paths such as `tooltipClassName`; no remaining `zIndexBase`, `overlayClassName`, or layering-only numeric `z-[...]` overrides are required on Dialog, Select, Popover, Menu, Tooltip, AlertDialog, or HoverCard consumers.
No centralized overlay wrapper under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` retains hardcoded numeric z-index classes or literals, including plain Tailwind utilities such as `z-50` on menu positioners.
Integration tests cover `open A -> open B -> close A -> close B` for both Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog, and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.

Tests validate menu submenu ownership, the overlay identity bridge, the lightbox precedence path, the non-modal opt-out semantics for tooltip/preview-card, and the shared marker-helper compatibility contracts that replace the old DOM-z-index ordering strategy.
The overlay-stack migration passes `npx tsc --noEmit`.
`npm test` passes after the overlay migration.
Public wrapper entrypoints remain import-compatible for existing consumers where practical, while removing only the layering-specific escape hatches that conflict with central ownership.

Deferred manual checks pass in a real app session: nested LoRA modal plus Model Select closes cleanly, drag interactions do not spuriously dismiss overlays, and focus trap / Escape / restore behavior feels correct.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Overlay validation: the broader Step 12 repo-wide layering grep is now too coarse to act as a clean invariant because it searches for any plain numeric Tailwind z-index utility across `src/shared/components`, `src/domains`, and `src/tools`. Running the equivalent command today returns many unrelated layout hits such as `src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx:133`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:606`, and numerous timeline/gallery chrome elements, so that check cannot distinguish forbidden migrated-overlay escapes from intentional non-overlay layering without manual triage.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Overlay validation: the broader Step 12 repo-wide layering grep is now too coarse to act as a clean invariant because it searches for any plain numeric Tailwind z-index utility across `src/shared/components`, `src/domains`, and `src/tools`. Running the equivalent command today returns many unrelated layout hits such as `src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx:133`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:606`, and numerous timeline/gallery chrome elements, so that check cannot distinguish forbidden migrated-overlay escapes from intentional non-overlay layering without manual triage.

rationale: The plan is ready to execute. Score has dropped to 1.5 after 10 iterations, there are no recurring critiques, preflight is clean, and the lone remaining significant flag is about one validation command being too coarse, not about subsystem choice, technical correctness, or missing migration scope. The plan already narrows the actual implementation and invariant surface to the centralized overlay wrappers, menu submenu branches, lightbox migration, helper cleanup, and call-site escape-hatch removal. That is sufficient to move forward without another rewrite cycle.
signals_assessment: Weighted score improved from 20.5 to 1.5 and then stabilized, with 29 resolved flags and only 1 open significant flag. The remaining flag is completeness-oriented and limited to validation ergonomics for a repo-wide z-index grep, not the core design. Preflight is fully green: project exists, is writable, criteria are present, and executor tooling is available. Given the low score, lack of reopened critiques, and clean preflight posture, execution should continue now.
warnings: Do not treat the broad repo-wide numeric z-index grep as a literal pass/fail invariant for the whole repository; it will include intentional non-overlay layout layers.
During execution, rely on the wrapper-only z-index invariant and targeted migrated-overlay consumer checks as the hard gates for layering centralization.
Manual runtime behaviors in the deferred-human list must be reported as `skipped` immediately if encountered; do not block or retry them in sandbox.

settled_decisions.decision: `ContextMenu` and `Drawer` remain explicit verified N/A families unless a real Base UI owner appears during execution.
settled_decisions.rationale: Repo inspection already established no `@base-ui/react/context-menu` or `@base-ui/react/drawer` usage under `src/`, and the visible drawer-like `ToolsPaneDrawer` is bespoke and out of scope.

settled_decisions.decision: Tooltip and preview-card participate in ordering/layer assignment only, not modal body-lock or focus restoration.
settled_decisions.rationale: This is now an explicit implementation invariant in the plan and should not be reopened during review.

settled_decisions.decision: Overlay-stack identity/topmost queries replace DOM-marker and computed-z-index precedence for migrated flows.
settled_decisions.rationale: The plan explicitly retires `targetHasHigherZIndex()` and related DOM/z-index precedence helpers for lightbox and Lora flows, avoiding dual ownership.

settled_decisions.decision: Both `DropdownMenuContent` and `DropdownMenuSubContent` must register with the stack and must not retain hardcoded numeric layering.
settled_decisions.rationale: The submenu branch is a distinct portal/positioner path and was called out explicitly to prevent partial menu migration.

orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea:
M2 — Overlay stack. Centralize Base UI portal ownership to harden against the class of bugs that caused the Model Select flash-close (and will re-emerge through other triggers if the mechanism isn't hardened).

## Executor rules

EXECUTOR HARD RULE (authoritative): For any task whose satisfaction requires a browser, runtime app instance, device, or manual human input that the sandboxed executor cannot produce (examples: telemetry overlay capture from a running app, drag/resize smoke checks, keyboard/playback behavior, AgentChat bridge manual test, pending-add URL flow verification, Model Select click behavior, textarea typing verification, any rendering count measured at runtime) — return status=skipped on FIRST encounter with executor_notes evidence naming the blocker (e.g. "no browser-driving path available"). DO NOT return status=blocked. DO NOT retry the task expecting different sandbox capabilities. The review/gate phase already marks these criteria as deferred_human; skipped tasks are first-class evidence that the criterion is deferred to post-milestone human verification. Blocked retries waste iteration budget and do not change outcome.

## Motivation

M1 → M1c eliminated the known render-storm triggers that caused Base UI overlay portals to unmount before their click-outside detection settled. That fixed the immediate Model Select / textarea-typing bugs. But the underlying mechanism — ad-hoc ownership of `body.pointer-events: none`, per-portal focus restoration, and uncoordinated z-index stacking across ~20 portal primitives — still exists. Any future render-storm trigger (new hook, new provider, new lib upgrade) can re-express the same bug class.

M2 hardens the mechanism itself: centralize overlay lifecycle so bugs in this class have one place to fix instead of twenty.

## Goal

Build a single overlay-stack store that wraps every Base UI portal primitive and owns:
1. `document.body.style.pointerEvents` — only the outermost open overlay sets it to 'none'; close order does not matter.
2. Focus restoration — central stack tracks which element had focus at overlay open; restores on close in reverse order.
3. Z-index layering — overlays consult the stack for their layer; no hardcoded z-index across files.

## Scope

### In scope

- `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` — Zustand store (using `createEntityStore` factory pattern where appropriate, or a simpler slice if entity-keyed isn't a fit).
- Wrapper components in `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` for each Base UI portal primitive:
  - Dialog, Popover, Select, Menu, Tooltip-with-modal, Drawer, AlertDialog, ContextMenu
- Migrate every callsite to the wrapper. Inventory command:
  `rg 'from .*@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup)' src/ --type ts -l`
  Expect 20+ callsites; actual count must match inventory at milestone start.

### Out of scope

- Replacing Base UI itself.
- Adding new overlay types.
- Touching overlay styling beyond what centralization requires.
- Writing E2E tests (no browser harness in repo).

## Dependencies (technical, not temporal)

- The M1-M1c store substrate must exist in the worktree: specifically `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` and `src/shared/state/selectionStore.ts`. Verify at milestone start via `ls src/shared/state/`.
- React 18+ (already in repo).
- Base UI portal primitives (already in repo).

## Implementation approach

1. **Inventory** all portal callsites via the grep above. Document each with its current z-index, focus-restoration behavior (if any), and pointer-events ownership.
2. **Build `overlayStack.ts`** — store with actions: `pushOverlay({ id, type, restoreFocusTo })`, `popOverlay(id)`, `getTopOverlay()`. Selector: `useIsOverlayTopmost(id)`.
3. **Build wrapper components** — one per primitive. Each wrapper:
   - Calls `pushOverlay` on mount; `popOverlay` on unmount.
   - Reads `useIsOverlayTopmost(id)` to decide whether to own body pointer-events.
   - Uses stack-layered z-index via a CSS variable pegged to stack depth.
4. **Migrate callsites** — one primitive type at a time. Keep prop API identical so consumer code doesn't change. Delete unused per-callsite z-index overrides and focus-management code as wrappers land.
5. **Regression harness** — integration tests (RTL/Vitest) that open/close overlay sequences and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after all overlays close.

## MUST (all automated)

- `npx tsc --noEmit` clean on the branch.
- `npm test` passes at M0/M1c baseline count or higher.
- No portal primitive imports remain outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` except in the wrappers themselves. Verify: `rg 'from .*@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup)' src/ --type ts -l | grep -v 'ui/overlay/'` returns empty.
- Integration tests covering open-A → open-B → close-A → close-B sequence for at least Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog pairings. Asserts `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.
- `overlayStack.ts` exports a public API documented with JSDoc on each action.
- No `z-index: [number]` literals in any migrated overlay wrapper file; all layering flows through the stack.

## DEFERRED_HUMAN (skipped by executor, verified manually post-milestone)

- Nested overlay sequences: LoRA modal → Model Select inside it; close LoRA → Model Select should close cleanly with no focus stolen, no body pointer-events stuck.
- Drag-while-overlay-open: open any overlay, drag a clip in timeline — overlay stays open, drag works, no click-outside false-positive dismissing overlay.
- Tab key traversal inside overlay stays trapped; Escape closes topmost; focus returns to opener.

## Non-goals / explicit choices

- No migration of non-Base-UI overlays (our own bespoke modals) unless they already use the same portal plumbing.
- No accessibility audit beyond focus-restoration correctness (different milestone if needed).
- No animation changes — this milestone is plumbing, not polish.

## Risk notes

- 20+ callsite migration is the largest-surface milestone in the chain. If a wrapper diverges from Base UI's prop API, all consumers of that primitive break. Keep wrappers thin.
- `document.body.style.pointerEvents` is a shared global; any non-migrated Base UI primitive opened during this milestone could conflict with the stack. Recommend landing all primitives in one PR.

Running the equivalent command today returns many unrelated layout hits such as `src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx:133`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:606`, and numerous timeline/gallery chrome elements, so that check cannot distinguish forbidden migrated-overlay escapes from intentional non-overlay layering without manual triage.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay layering: Recurring debt: call-site z-index overrides remain outside the migration contract, so central wrapper ownership would still be bypassed by `TooltipContent`, `SelectContent`, and `DialogContent` consumers that currently pass `z-[...]`, `overlayClassName`, or `zIndexBase`.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the revision around the real root cause: the plan now treats wrapper migration, caller z-index escape-hatch cleanup, and retirement of lightbox DOM-precedence helpers as one coordinated change. It also explicitly includes the existing `hover-card` portal wrapper, names the supporting files and tests that must move with the stack, and makes caller contract preservation versus layering-override removal concrete.

resolved_flags.concern: Lightbox overlay ownership: the plan does not explicitly retire the existing DOM-z-index precedence helpers, leaving `overlayStack` and `uiLayers` as competing sources of truth for which overlay is topmost.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the milestone brief against the actual overlay surface in `src/shared/components/ui/*` and the plan's proposed migration list. The repo still has a portal-bearing `hover-card.tsx` built on `@base-ui/react/preview-card` with a hardcoded `z-[100001]`, and that component is used in live lightbox-adjacent flows such as `AdjacentSegmentNavigation.tsx`, `ConstituentImageNavigation.tsx`, and `VariantCard.tsx`; the plan raises this in metadata as an open question instead of resolving whether it is intentionally out of scope, so the centralization brief is only partially addressed.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the revision around the real root cause: the plan now treats wrapper migration, caller z-index escape-hatch cleanup, and retirement of lightbox DOM-precedence helpers as one coordinated change. It also explicitly includes the existing `hover-card` portal wrapper, names the supporting files and tests that must move with the stack, and makes caller contract preservation versus layering-override removal concrete.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the user requirement that z-index ownership stop being hardcoded across files, not just inside wrappers. Current call sites still override overlay layering directly through props and classes, for example `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` uses `overlayClassName="!z-[100012]"` and `zIndexBase={UI_Z_LAYERS.LIGHTBOX_MODAL + 2}`, `sharedSections.tsx` passes `className="z-[100001]"` to `SelectContent`, and many `TooltipContent` callers in the lightbox and variant selector pass `z-[100001]` or `z-[100010]`; the plan does not make those call-site overrides a first-class cleanup target, so it only partially satisfies the approved requirement that layering flow through the central stack.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the revision around the real root cause: the plan now treats wrapper migration, caller z-index escape-hatch cleanup, and retirement of lightbox DOM-precedence helpers as one coordinated change. It also explicitly includes the existing `hover-card` portal wrapper, names the supporting files and tests that must move with the stack, and makes caller contract preservation versus layering-override removal concrete.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the current nested-modal path in `LoraSelectorModal.tsx`. That caller depends on `DialogContent`'s `zIndexBase` and `overlayClassName` escape hatches to sit above the lightbox, and it closes itself only when `getTopDialogElement() === contentRef.current`; the plan says those z-index escape hatches should probably be removed and wrapper-local layering should come from the new stack, but it never explains how this specific caller will keep its above-lightbox ordering or how the top-dialog check will continue to work once the rendered topmost element may no longer be the same content node reference.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the revision around the real root cause: the plan now treats wrapper migration, caller z-index escape-hatch cleanup, and retirement of lightbox DOM-precedence helpers as one coordinated change. It also explicitly includes the existing `hover-card` portal wrapper, names the supporting files and tests that must move with the stack, and makes caller contract preservation versus layering-override removal concrete.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the existing lightbox interaction logic in `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `uiLayers.ts`. Those paths currently gate outside-click and Escape behavior by scanning DOM elements marked with `data-dialog-backdrop`, `data-dialog-content`, and `data-lightbox-popup` and comparing computed z-index values; if M2 introduces `overlayStack` as the new source of truth but leaves these DOM-scanning guards in place, the app will have two competing overlay-order mechanisms, which is a correctness risk rather than just an implementation detail.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the revision around the real root cause: the plan now treats wrapper migration, caller z-index escape-hatch cleanup, and retirement of lightbox DOM-precedence helpers as one coordinated change. It also explicitly includes the existing `hover-card` portal wrapper, names the supporting files and tests that must move with the stack, and makes caller contract preservation versus layering-override removal concrete.

resolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. The plan's assumption that most consumers can stay untouched behind compatibility re-exports is optimistic because many of the current behavior differences live in those callers through `container`, `className`, and z-index overrides, so the broader issue is not confined to moving raw Base UI imports into one folder.

It also explicitly includes the existing `hover-card` portal wrapper, names the supporting files and tests that must move with the stack, and makes caller contract preservation versus layering-override removal concrete.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the raw-import invariant against all current `src/` matches. The revised plan accounts for `command.tsx` and the wrapper/lightbox owners, but it still does not mention `src/shared/components/ui/tooltipPrimitives.test.tsx`, whose `vi.mock('@base-ui/react/tooltip', ...)` line will continue to trip the stated 'no raw imports remain anywhere under `src/` outside `ui/overlay/`' grep unless tests are explicitly exempted or updated.
resolved_flags.resolution: Patched the plan surgically: corrected the raw-import inventory to include the tooltip test mock, added explicit cleanup for that test and the `GuidanceVideoStrip` helper path, extended layering cleanup to forwarded `tooltipClassName` callers, and made non-modal tooltip/preview-card overlays opt out of focus restoration and body-lock ownership.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the popup-marker helper surface beyond the files named in Step 8. `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/GuidanceVideoStrip.tsx` still has two open-coded guards on `button, select, [role="listbox"]` around strip click and drag-start handling, but the revised plan's helper-centralization list does not include this file even though it participates in the same popup-interaction boundary logic as `useSlidingPane.ts` and `elementPolicy.ts`.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the real caller paths for layering overrides, not just direct `TooltipContent` / `HoverCardContent` JSX. `src/shared/components/selectors/ShotSelectorWithAdd.tsx` still passes `tooltipClassName="z-[100001]"` into `ShotPrimaryActionButton`, and `src/shared/components/shots/ShotPrimaryActionButton.tsx` forwards that prop into `TooltipContent`; the revised plan's cleanup examples focus on direct overlay consumers, so this indirect prop path is still easy to miss unless the grep-driven migration explicitly follows wrapper props as well as direct JSX sites.
resolved_flags.resolution: Patched the plan surgically: corrected the raw-import inventory to include the tooltip test mock, added explicit cleanup for that test and the `GuidanceVideoStrip` helper path, extended layering cleanup to forwarded `tooltipClassName` callers, and made non-modal tooltip/preview-card overlays opt out of focus restoration and body-lock ownership.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay stack: `LightboxShell.tsx` remains a live raw `@base-ui/react/dialog` owner, but the revised steps still do not explicitly migrate that file into the centralized overlay wrapper layer even though the success criteria forbid raw portal imports outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised the plan to explicitly migrate `LightboxShell` through a sanctioned overlay wrapper path, preserve and centralize marker-based `container` resolution, and include the concrete helper callers and type-only raw import cleanup needed to satisfy the no-raw-import and single-source-of-truth goals.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay stack: helper-based `container` callers still depend on `[data-dialog-content]` / `[data-lightbox-popup]` DOM markers, but the migration plan does not state that those markers remain stable or list the helper files that resolve containers through them.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised the plan to explicitly migrate `LightboxShell` through a sanctioned overlay wrapper path, preserve and centralize marker-based `container` resolution, and include the concrete helper callers and type-only raw import cleanup needed to satisfy the no-raw-import and single-source-of-truth goals.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Overlay scope: the revised plan fixed the same omission for `ContextMenu`, but it still does not explicitly reconcile the brief's `Drawer` wrapper requirement with the actual repo surface. There is no Base UI drawer primitive under `src/`, and the only drawer-like surface I found is the custom `ToolsPaneDrawer` in `src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx`, so the plan should state whether Drawer is another verified N/A family or whether that bespoke drawer is intentionally out of scope under the brief's non-goals.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the revision to the remaining execution gaps: the plan now makes `Drawer` an explicit verified N/A for this repo because the only drawer-like surface is bespoke and out of scope, and it explicitly requires the menu submenu portal branch (`DropdownMenuSubContent`) to migrate into the overlay stack alongside top-level menu content.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Overlay precedence: `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts` still has a separate `targetHasHigherZIndex()` path that walks ancestors and compares computed `z-index` values against `UI_Z_LAYERS.LIGHTBOX_MODAL`. Step 7 says this hook will stop using `hasDialogAbove()` and move to stack-based topmost checks, but it never says how this second computed-style branch is removed or replaced, so the plan can satisfy its named migration while still leaving one DOM/z-index precedence mechanism in the lightbox path.
resolved_flags.resolution: Patched the remaining gaps without changing direction: the plan now makes ContextMenu an explicit repository-level N/A decision backed by grep, and it explicitly deletes the lightbox `targetHasHigherZIndex()` computed-z-index branch so stack-based precedence is the only remaining mechanism.

resolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the raw-owner inventory in the current repo with the same Base UI grep the brief cites. There are 9 matches today (`LightboxShell.tsx` plus the existing shared wrapper files like `dialog.tsx`, `popover.tsx`, `select.tsx`, `dropdown-menu.tsx`, `tooltipPrimitives.tsx`, `alert-dialog.tsx`, `hover-card.tsx`, and the type-only `command.tsx` import), so the repository is already mostly centralized at the wrapper layer. That makes the plan's repeated framing around '20+ callsites' and large one-primitive-at-a-time downstream callsite migration stale relative to the actual repo surface and risks unnecessary churn outside the remaining raw-owner and helper hotspots.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the plan to the repo’s real overlay surface, made Base UI containment explicit, changed registration from mount/unmount to true open/present lifecycle, expanded the CSS marker inventory, and replaced example-based z-index cleanup with a grep-driven overlay-consumer migration plan.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay markers: backdrop-marker compatibility is still under-specified; `useLightboxTransition.ts` and lightbox transition CSS depend on `[data-dialog-backdrop]`, but the revised plan only makes `data-dialog-content` and `data-lightbox-popup` stability explicit.
resolved_flags.resolution: Extended the plan's compatibility contract to explicitly preserve and migrate backdrop-marker behavior, naming the live `[data-dialog-backdrop]` consumers, related CSS, and the shared helper work needed so wrapper centralization does not break lightbox transition cleanup or other marker-based flows.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay identity propagation: the plan replaces DOM topmost checks with `overlayStack` selectors, but current Lora and lightbox callers do not receive a wrapper-generated overlay id or context, so the migration path for `getTopDialogElement()` / `hasDialogAbove()` is still under-specified.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised the plan to add an explicit overlay-identity bridge for existing caller shapes, and expanded the migration checklist to cover popup-marker-dependent helpers like `elementPolicy.ts` and `useMobileInteractions.ts` without widening M2 beyond the overlay ownership subsystem.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay interaction helpers: `elementPolicy.ts` and `useMobileInteractions.ts` still hardcode popup/menu DOM markers, but the revised plan does not explicitly preserve or migrate those supporting paths alongside the new overlay wrappers.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised the plan to add an explicit overlay-identity bridge for existing caller shapes, and expanded the migration checklist to cover popup-marker-dependent helpers like `elementPolicy.ts` and `useMobileInteractions.ts` without widening M2 beyond the overlay ownership subsystem.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay ownership: the plan still does not state how Base UI's own body pointer-events and focus-restoration behavior will be disabled or contained, so the proposed overlay stack may end up fighting the library instead of becoming the sole owner.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the plan to the repo’s real overlay surface, made Base UI containment explicit, changed registration from mount/unmount to true open/present lifecycle, expanded the CSS marker inventory, and replaced example-based z-index cleanup with a grep-driven overlay-consumer migration plan.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay migration scope: the plan still assumes a 20+ direct-callsite migration even though the current repo has only 9 raw Base UI overlay-import sites and is already mostly centralized through shared wrapper files.
resolved_flags.resolution: `rg 'from .*@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card)' src/ --type ts -l` returns 9 files in this checkout, most of them existing wrapper implementations under `src/shared/components/ui/`.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay layering cleanup: the revised plan still does not enumerate the full caller surface for numeric z-index overrides, but its validation grep expects those overrides to be gone across broad directories.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the plan to the repo’s real overlay surface, made Base UI containment explicit, changed registration from mount/unmount to true open/present lifecycle, expanded the CSS marker inventory, and replaced example-based z-index cleanup with a grep-driven overlay-consumer migration plan.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay import inventory: the revised plan's '9 current matches' baseline undercounts `src/shared/components/ui/tooltipPrimitives.test.tsx`, which still imports `@base-ui/react/tooltip`; the stated raw-import invariant under `src/` will fail unless test files are explicitly scoped out or migrated.
resolved_flags.resolution: Patched the plan surgically: corrected the raw-import inventory to include the tooltip test mock, added explicit cleanup for that test and the `GuidanceVideoStrip` helper path, extended layering cleanup to forwarded `tooltipClassName` callers, and made non-modal tooltip/preview-card overlays opt out of focus restoration and body-lock ownership.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay helper coverage: `GuidanceVideoStrip.tsx` still uses open-coded popup/listbox guards, but the revised helper-centralization step does not include that file even though it participates in the same popup-interaction boundary logic as the named helper consumers.
resolved_flags.resolution: `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/GuidanceVideoStrip.tsx:231` and `:387` both check `closest('button, select, [role="listbox"]')`, while Step 8 names other popup-marker helper consumers but not this file.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay precedence: `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts` still contains `targetHasHigherZIndex()`, a separate computed-z-index gate that Step 7 does not explicitly retire, so the plan can leave one DOM/z-index precedence branch in place even after migrating `hasDialogAbove()` callers.
resolved_flags.resolution: Patched the remaining gaps without changing direction: the plan now makes ContextMenu an explicit repository-level N/A decision backed by grep, and it explicitly deletes the lightbox `targetHasHigherZIndex()` computed-z-index branch so stack-based precedence is the only remaining mechanism.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay scope: the plan now explicitly reconciles ContextMenu with the actual repo, but it still does not say how the brief's Drawer family maps to this checkout, where there is no Base UI drawer primitive and the only drawer-like surface is the custom `ToolsPaneDrawer` in `src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx`.
resolved_flags.resolution: Repo search found no `@base-ui/react/drawer` or Base UI drawer owner under `src/`, but it did find a custom fixed-position drawer implementation in `src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx:170-256`; the revised plan names ContextMenu-as-N/A but never makes the same explicit scope decision for Drawer.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay callers: menu submenu ownership remains implicit even though `DropdownMenuSubContent` is a separate `MenuPrimitive.Portal`/`Positioner` branch with its own hardcoded z-index path.
resolved_flags.resolution: Narrowed the revision to the remaining execution gaps: the plan now makes `Drawer` an explicit verified N/A for this repo because the only drawer-like surface is bespoke and out of scope, and it explicitly requires the menu submenu portal branch (`DropdownMenuSubContent`) to migrate into the overlay stack alongside top-level menu content.

resolved_flags.concern: Overlay validation: Step 12's cleanup grep does not search for plain numeric Tailwind classes such as `z-50`, so the plan can claim wrapper-layering cleanup while still missing the current hardcoded menu positioner layers.
resolved_flags.resolution: The current wrappers still use `className="z-50"` in `src/shared/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx:58` and `:106`, but the proposed validation command only matches `z-\[[0-9]+\]`, `tooltipClassName`, `overlayClassName`, and `zIndexBase`.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 10. Weighted score trajectory: 20.5 -> 12.0 -> 10.0 -> 10.5 -> 13.5 -> 10.0 -> 5.5 -> 5.0 -> 1.5 -> 1.5. Plan deltas: 88.1%, 95.0%, 63.3%, 85.7%, 74.1%, 89.7%, 6.8%, 86.7%, 2.7%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 29. Open significant flags: 1.

flag_resolutions.rationale: The concern is real: the broad Step 12 grep over `src/shared/components`, `src/domains`, and `src/tools` will surface unrelated layout z-index utilities such as `SelectionContextMenu.tsx` and `AgentChat.tsx`, so it is not a clean repo-wide invariant by itself. That does not justify another planning loop because the plan already contains narrower, execution-relevant checks that match the actual success criteria: a wrapper-only grep for `src/shared/components/ui/overlay`, explicit migrated-overlay escape-hatch cleanup (`tooltipClassName`, `overlayClassName`, `zIndexBase`), and raw Base UI import elimination outside `ui/overlay/`. Execution should treat the coarse grep as exploratory inventory, not a blocking invariant.
